@AGENTS.md

# Irrlicht — Development Guide

## Project Structure

- `core/` — Go daemon and CLI tools (module: `irrlicht/core`)
- `platforms/` — Different frontends like Web and Swift
- `site/` — Landing page and documentation (static HTML, GitHub Pages)

## Build Artifacts

Use `./.build` for build artifacts.

## Key Conventions

- Go code follows hexagonal architecture: `domain/` → `ports/` → `adapters/` → `application/services/`
- Three session states only: `working`, `waiting`, `ready` — no cancelled state
- Errors are logged via `Logger` interface, not propagated with `fmt.Errorf`
- Child sessions (subagents and background agents) use `ParentSessionID` for parent-child linking

{"assignees":[],"body":"## Summary\n\nIrrlicht supports the classic Codex transcript schema, but does not fully support the newer wrapped Codex schema used by current `codex` sessions from VS Code, Desktop, and `codex exec`.\n\nThis causes incomplete or incorrect monitoring for a significant subset of real Codex sessions.\n\n## Problem\n\nThe current Codex parser in `core/adapters/inbound/agents/codex/parser.go` handles classic top-level events like:\n\n- `message`\n- `function_call`\n- `function_call_output`\n- `reasoning`\n\nBut many current Codex sessions use a wrapped schema with events such as:\n\n- `session_meta`\n- `turn_context`\n- `event_msg`\n- `response_item`\n\nAnd nested payload types such as:\n\n- `response_item.payload.type == \"message\"`\n- `response_item.payload.type == \"function_call\"`\n- `response_item.payload.type == \"function_call_output\"`\n- `response_item.payload.type == \"custom_tool_call\"`\n- `response_item.payload.type == \"custom_tool_call_output\"`\n- `response_item.payload.type == \"web_search_call\"`\n\nIrrlicht currently skips or only partially extracts these wrapped events.\n\n## Impact\n\nFor wrapped Codex sessions, Irrlicht currently misses important monitoring signals:\n\n- CWD / workdir is not recovered reliably\n- assistant text is not extracted for waiting-state heuristics\n- wrapped tool calls are not tracked as open/closed tools\n- custom tools like `apply_patch` are ignored\n- web search tool activity is ignored\n- session source/client distinctions like `exec`, `vscode`, and `Codex Desktop` are not surfaced\n\nThis means sessions may appear with degraded metadata and incorrect state transitions, especially for newer Codex clients.\n\n## Evidence\n\nLocal audit of real `~/.codex/sessions/**/*.jsonl` files:\n\n- `77` total Codex session files\n- `47` classic-only sessions\n- `30` wrapped-only sessions\n\nClassic sessions are mostly handled correctly:\n\n- CWD found in `47/47`\n- tool-using sessions tracked in `37/37`\n\nWrapped sessions are not:\n\n- CWD missing in `30/30`\n- assistant text missing in `30/30`\n- wrapped tool events present in `25` sessions, tracked in `0/25`\n\nObserved wrapped session sources included:\n\n- `source = exec`\n- `source = vscode`\n- `originator = Codex Desktop`\n\n## Suggested Scope\n\nUpdate Codex support so Irrlicht can normalize both classic and wrapped Codex transcript formats.\n\nLikely work:\n\n- parse `session_meta.payload.cwd` and related metadata\n- parse `turn_context.payload.cwd`, sandbox, approval, and model metadata where useful\n- unwrap `response_item` payloads and map them into the existing normalized `ParsedEvent`\n- support wrapped assistant/user messages from `response_item.payload.type == \"message\"`\n- support wrapped tool calls/results from `function_call` / `function_call_output`\n- decide how to represent `custom_tool_call`, `custom_tool_call_output`, and `web_search_call`\n- add tests covering both classic and wrapped real-world Codex transcripts\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n\n- wrapped Codex sessions correctly recover CWD\n- wrapped assistant messages populate `LastAssistantText`\n- wrapped tool calls/results affect open-tool tracking correctly\n- state classification works for wrapped sessions the same way it does for classic sessions\n- real sample transcripts from current Codex clients are covered by tests\n\n## Related\n\n- Related to #81, but this is specific enough to warrant its own implementation content.text:
Implemented issue `#90` on branch `issue-90-wrapped-codex-schema` and opened PR `#92`.

**What Changed**
- **Codex parser:** `core/adapters/inbound/agents/codex/parser.go` now unwraps wrapped `response_item` payloads for `message`, `function_call`, `function_call_output`, `custom_tool_call`, `custom_tool_call_output`, and `web_search_call`.
- **CWD recovery:** `core/pkg/transcript/cwd.go` now reads wrapped `session_meta.payload.cwd`, `turn_context.payload.cwd`, wrapped content blocks, and wrapped tool-call `arguments.workdir`.
- **Shared extraction:** `core/pkg/tailer/parser.go` now reads wrapped `payload.content`, `payload.arguments`, `payload.output`, and wrapped assistant text.

**Tests**
- **Added:** wrapped-schema regression tests in `core/adapters/inbound/agents/codex/parser_test.go`.
- **Added:** wrapped Codex transcript CWD coverage in `core/adapters/outbound/git/adapter_test.go`.
- **Passed:** `cd core && go test ./adapters/inbound/agents/codex ./adapters/outbound/git ./pkg/transcript ./pkg/tailer ./application/services`
- **Note:** `cd core && go test ./...` still hits an existing `cmd/irrlichd` setup failure because `cmd/irrlichd/main.go` embeds `ui` but no matching files exist in this worktree.

**PR**
- **URL:** `https://github.com/ingo-eichhorst/Irrlicht/pull/92`

If you want, I can also add a short changelog entry for this fix.

fix 

  Code Review — PR #92: Support newer wrapped Codex transcript schema                               
                                                                                                    
  PR: ingo-eichhorst/Irrlicht#92 | +372 / -76 | All tests pass                                      
                                                                                                    
  Overview                                                                                          
                                                                                                    
  This PR adds support for the newer "wrapped" Codex transcript schema where events are nested      
  inside response_item payloads rather than appearing at the top level. Addresses a real gap: 30/77 
  observed Codex sessions used the wrapped format and were getting zero CWD, assistant text, or tool
   tracking.                                                                                        
                                                                                                 
  What's good                                                                                       
                                                                                                    
  - Clean decomposition: The monolithic ParseLine switch is broken into focused helpers             
  (parseCodexMessage, parseCodexResponseItem, parseCodexFunctionCall, parseCodexFunctionCallOutput) 
  — much easier to follow and test individually.                                                    
  - Reuse of existing infrastructure: Wrapped parsing delegates to the same helpers as classic      
  parsing (parseCodexMessage handles both "message" events and response_item.payload.type ==        
  "message"), avoiding duplication.                                                                 
  - CWD extraction consolidated into pkg/transcript: The old extractCodexCWD was removed from the   
  parser and replaced with the shared transcript.ExtractCWDFromLine, following the project          
  convention that format-specific parsers belong in adapters but shared utilities belong in pkg/.   
  - Thorough test coverage: The full-transcript integration test                                    
  (TestParser_FullWrappedTranscript_MetadataAndWaitingState) covers the entire pipeline from JSONL  
  through tailer through state classification — exactly the kind of end-to-end test this change     
  needs.                                                                                            
  - Follows project conventions: 3 states only, no fmt.Errorf, hexagonal boundaries respected.      
                                                                                                    
  Issues & suggestions                                                                              
                                                                                                    
  1. parseCodexFunctionCall rejects calls without a name — behavior change                          
                                                                                                    
  func parseCodexFunctionCall(raw map[string]interface{}, ev *tailer.ParsedEvent) bool {            
        name, _ := raw["name"].(string)                                                             
        if name == "" {                                                                             
                return false                                                                        
        }                                                                                           
                                                                                                 
  Previously, a function_call with no name field would still produce an event with EventType =      
  "function_call" and an empty ToolUseNames. Now it's skipped entirely. This is probably the right  
  call (nameless tool calls aren't useful), but it's a subtle behavior change worth noting. Are     
  there real Codex transcripts where name is absent on a function_call? If so, this would stop      
  tracking them as activity.                                                                     

  2. web_search_call mapped to function_call_output — intentional?                                  
                                                                                                    
  case "web_search_call":                                                                           
        ev.EventType = "function_call_output"                                                       
        ev.ToolUseNames = []string{"web_search"}                                                    
        ev.ToolResultCount = 1                                                                      
                                                                                                    
  This maps the call to function_call_output (a result type), not function_call. This means the web 
  search doesn't increment the open tool counter and then decrement it — it only fires a "result"   
  event. The test confirms this is intentional, but it means web_search will never appear in        
  LastOpenToolNames during execution. If Codex doesn't emit a separate web_search_result, this is   
  fine — it's a single atomic event. Just flagging for awareness.                                

  3. ExtractAssistantText checks raw["payload"]["content"] at the shared level                      
                                                                                                    
  In tailer/parser.go:130-134:                                                                      
  // Wrapped Codex: payload.content[]                                                               
  if payload, ok := raw["payload"].(map[string]interface{}); ok {                                   
      if arr, ok := payload["content"].([]interface{}); ok {                                        
          collectText(arr)                                                                          
      }                                                                                             
  }                                                                                                 
                                                                                                    
  This is in the shared ExtractAssistantText helper, meaning Claude Code and Pi parsers will also   
  attempt to read payload.content[]. In practice this is harmless (those formats don't have a       
  payload key), but it's a slight leak of Codex-specific knowledge into the shared layer. Consider  
  whether the Codex parser should unwrap the payload before calling ExtractAssistantText instead.   
                                                                                                    
  Same concern applies to ExtractContentChars which now reads payload.arguments, payload.output, and
   payload.message.                                                                                 
                                                                                                    
  4. extractCWDFromArguments no longer gate on type == "function_call"                              
                                                                                                 
  The old code only parsed arguments for CWD when raw["type"] == "function_call". The new           
  extractCWDFromArguments parses any event's arguments field. This is slightly more permissive — if 
  some other event type had an arguments field with a workdir key, it would now be picked up. Low   
  risk in practice, but worth a comment.                                                            
                                                                                                 
  5. Minor: the transcript package has no test files                                                
                                                                                                    
  ?   irrlicht/core/pkg/transcript  [no test files]                                                 
                                                                                                    
  The CWD extraction logic got meaningfully more complex (now 6 sources checked in priority order). 
  A targeted unit test for ExtractCWDFromLine with classic vs. wrapped inputs would add confidence, 
  especially since this is shared infrastructure. The git adapter test covers it end-to-end, but a  
  unit test would catch regressions faster.                                                         
                                                                                                 
  6. Import of application/services in parser test

  parser_test.go imports application/services (for ClassifyState) to verify the state classification
   result. This creates an upward dependency from an adapter test to the application layer. It works
   and tests the right thing, but in strict hexagonal terms, the adapter test is reaching up.       
  Consider whether this assertion could live in a services test instead, or accept the pragmatic    
  trade-off (it's a test file, not production code).                                             

  Verdict

  This is a solid, well-tested PR that addresses a real monitoring gap for ~40% of observed Codex   
  sessions. The refactoring of the parser into composable helpers is a clear improvement. The       
  wrapped schema support follows the existing patterns well.                                        
                                                                                                    
  The main thing I'd want clarified is point #3 — whether leaking payload-awareness into the shared 
  ExtractAssistantText/ExtractContentChars helpers is the intended long-term design, or if the Codex
   parser should unwrap before calling shared helpers. Everything else is minor.                    
                                                                                                    
  Recommendation: Approve with optional nits.

  Compiles the Go daemon, builds the Swift app, kills all running instances
  (including production Irrlicht.app), and launches the freshly built versions.
  Use when the user says "test mac", "restart mac", "rebuild mac", or "/ir:test-mac".
---

# Build & Restart macOS Dev Stack

Build the irrlicht daemon and Swift app, then replace all running instances with the new builds.

## Steps

1. **Build the Go daemon**
   ```bash
   cd /Users/ingo/projects/irrlicht/core && go build -o bin/irrlichd ./cmd/irrlichd
   ```

2. **Build the Swift app**
   ```bash
   cd /Users/ingo/projects/irrlicht/platforms/macos && swift build 2>&1 | tail -5
   ```

3. **Kill all running irrlicht processes** (production app, production daemon, debug app, debug daemon)
   ```bash
   pkill -f "Irrlicht.app" 2>/dev/null
   pkill -f "\.build.*Irrlicht" 2>/dev/null
   pkill -f irrlichd 2>/dev/null
   sleep 2
   ```

4. **Clean up stale socket**
   ```bash
   rm -f /Users/ingo/.local/share/irrlicht/irrlichd.sock
   ```

5. **Start the dev daemon**
   ```bash
   cd /Users/ingo/projects/irrlicht/core && nohup ./bin/irrlichd > /tmp/irrlichd-dev.log 2>&1 & disown
   ```

6. **Wait for daemon to be ready** — confirm port 7837 is listening before launching the app
   ```bash
   sleep 2 && lsof -iTCP:7837 -sTCP:LISTEN -P -n 2>/dev/null
   ```

7. **Start the dev Swift app**
   ```bash
   nohup /Users/ingo/projects/irrlicht/platforms/macos/.build/arm64-apple-macosx/debug/Irrlicht > /tmp/irrlicht-app-dev.log 2>&1 & disown
   ```

8. **Verify** — confirm both processes are running and the daemon is serving sessions
   ```bash
   pgrep -f "bin/irrlichd" && pgrep -f "\.build.*Irrlicht" && curl -s http://localhost:7837/api/v1/sessions | head -1
   ```

## Notes
- The production Irrlicht.app (from DMG) bundles its own daemon. It MUST be killed before starting the dev daemon, otherwise port 7837 will be occupied.
- Daemon logs: `/tmp/irrlichd-dev.log`
- Swift app logs: `/tmp/irrlicht-app-dev.log`
